{"state_analysis":"The terminal is at the root directory of the Docker container, specifically at '/app'. ","is_blocking":true,"timeout_sec":5},{"keystrokes":"vim environment.yml
","is_blocking":false,"timeout_sec":0},{"keystrokes":"/tensorflow

","is_blocking":false,"timeout_sec":0},{"keystrokes":"n
","is_blocking":false,"timeout_sec":0},{"keystrokes":"/scipy
","is_blocking":false,"timeout_sec":0},{"keystrokes":"n
","is_blocking":false,"timeout_sec":0},{"keystrokes":":%s/scipy=1.5/scipy=1.6/g
","is_blocking":false,"timeout_sec":0},{"keystrokes":":wq
","is_blocking":false,"timeout_sec":0},{"keystrokes":"conda env create -f environment.yml -n datasci
","is_blocking":true,"timeout_sec":120}],"is_task_complete":false}

{"state_analysis":"The environment.yml file contains multiple conflicting dependencies. Specifically, TensorFlow 2.8.0 has compatibility issues with the current Python version and CUDA settings. Additionally, the required version of scikit-learn conflicts with the Python versions available for installation.","explanation":"We need to edit the environment.yml file to adjust the Python version and resolve the package conflicts by selecting compatible package versions. We will change TensorFlow to a version that does not conflict with Python 3.10 and adjust scikit-learn accordingly.","commands":[{"keystrokes":"vim environment.yml
","is_blocking":false,"timeout_sec":0},{"keystrokes":"/python
","is_blocking":false,"timeout_sec":0},{"keystrokes":"n

","is_blocking":false,"timeout_sec":0},{"keystrokes":"/scikit-learn
","is_blocking":false,"timeout_sec":0},{"keystrokes":":%s/scikit-learn=1.0.2/scikit-learn=1.0.1/g
","is_blocking":false,"timeout_sec":0},{"keystrokes":":wq
","is_blocking":false,"timeout_sec":0},{"keystrokes":"conda env create -f environment.yml -n datasci
","is_blocking":true,"timeout_sec":120}],"is_task_complete":false}

{"state_analysis":"The current terminal output indicates that there are still incompatibilities with Python versions and dependencies specified in the environment.yml file. The specific issues include:
1. TensorFlow 2.8.0 is not installable due to Python and CUDA version conflicts.
2. scikit-learn 1.0.2 has conflicts with Python 3.10, as it requires Python versions that are incompatible with other dependencies.","explanation":"We need to adjust the environment.yml file to choose package versions that can work with Python 3.10, which is preferred. The plan is to downgrade TensorFlow to a compatible version and adjust scikit-learn and Python versions accordingly.","commands":[{"keystrokes":"vim environment.yml

keystrokes.description: Keystrokes to execute in the terminal. Use tmux-style escape sequences for modifier keys (e.g. C-c for ctrl-c). Modifier keys must be sent as their own commands otherwise the characters will be interpreted literally.
keystrokes.title: Keystrokes

is_blocking.description: Whether to wait for and return the terminal output after executing these keystrokes. This will append '; tmux wait -S done' to your command.DO NOT block on modifier keys or inside interactive programs (e.g. vim or less). Only block when the command is executed in the command line, is not interactive, and you expect the output to be returned with no intervention. When in doubt, wait instead of blocking.
is_blocking.title: Is Blocking

timeout_sec.description: The number of expected seconds to wait for the command to complete.
timeout_sec.title: Timeout Sec

state_analysis.description: Description of the current state of the terminal
state_analysis.title: State Analysis

explanation.description: Brief explanation of what these commands will do
explanation.title: Explanation

commands.description: List of shell interactions to execute in the Docker container
commands.title: Commands

is_task_complete.description: Whether the task is complete following the execution of these commands. Make sure to check that the command you last executed worked before saying you're done.
is_task_complete.title: Is Task Complete
